model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The prior step underperformed because the scheduler configuration effectively spent almost all 256 steps in warmup. This candidate reverts scheduling and increases the Adam learning rate to improve early optimization under the fixed short-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Under a short fixed-step budget, decoupled weight decay can regularize more cleanly than Adam's coupled decay. Switching to AdamW with moderately stronger decay is a compact optimizer-family change that often improves validation loss on CIFAR-10 MLPs.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"A small amount of label smoothing is a low-complexity regularization change that often improves validation cross-entropy for CIFAR-10 classifiers, especially when training is short and prone to overconfident logits.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_BATCHNORM = True","new":"USE_BATCHNORM = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With only 256 update steps, BatchNorm running statistics can be noisy and hurt validation behavior in a flat MLP. Disabling BatchNorm and removing label smoothing returns to a cleaner optimization signal while testing a simpler architecture path.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_BATCHNORM = False","new":"USE_BATCHNORM = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-3","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The last run regressed heavily due to disabling BatchNorm; this restores the stronger prior configuration and then reduces AdamW decay to avoid underfitting in the short 256-step budget. Lower decoupled decay is a targeted optimizer-dynamics adjustment with low complexity.","secondary_modes":["layout","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 1","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With only 256 optimization steps, a shallower MLP can reach a better trained regime than a deeper one. Reducing depth is a minimal architecture simplification to improve optimization efficiency within the fixed budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 1","new":"DEPTH = 2","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-3","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Recent regressions came from architecture/optimizer drift away from the strongest baseline. This candidate restores the baseline-like Adam setup and tests a slightly higher learning rate (8e-4) to improve progress within 256 steps while keeping regularization moderate.","secondary_modes":["indexing","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With fixed training steps, doubling batch size exposes the model to more unique examples within the same step budget. Pairing this with a proportional LR bump keeps update magnitude in range and may improve short-horizon validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The successful jump came from larger effective data throughput per step plus higher LR. A small additional LR increase is a focused follow-up to test whether optimization can move faster within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"After finding a strong LR/batch regime, a small reduction in L2 regularization can improve fit within the short fixed-step horizon while keeping optimization behavior nearly unchanged.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 160","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The current best appears throughput-limited under fixed steps. A moderate batch-size increase can expose more samples per optimizer update while preserving most of the already-tuned optimization settings.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 160","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last two improvements came from increasing examples processed per step. Testing a further moderate increase to batch size is a direct continuation of that trend with minimal code complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Performance improved substantially as batch size increased under fixed-step evaluation, indicating sample-throughput is a key lever. Testing 256 continues that trend with a simple single-parameter change.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The optimization keeps improving with larger batches in fixed-step mode, suggesting underutilized sample throughput. Increasing to 320 is a controlled continuation to test if the trend still holds.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Validation loss continues to improve monotonically with larger batch sizes in this fixed-step regime. Increasing to 384 is the next incremental throughput test while preserving the successful optimizer settings.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Loss is improving strongly and consistently as batch size increases, indicating the fixed-step objective benefits from maximizing sample throughput. Moving to 512 is the next direct test of that scaling trend.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 640","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The run is still strongly throughput-driven with larger batches yielding better fixed-step validation loss. Increasing to 640 continues the same successful direction while keeping all other tuned settings unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 640","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Given continued gains at 640 despite longer wall time, a further batch-size increase is the most promising remaining single-parameter move for fixed-step loss minimization.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 896","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"At very large batches, gains are still positive but tapering; increasing learning rate alongside another batch increase can restore per-step progress while preserving the successful overall regime.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
